title: Judge Blocks Trump Administration from Withholding Funds for EV Charger Infrastructure
content: A U.S. judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the Trump administration from withholding billions in EV charger funding from 14 states, including California, New York, Illinois, and Washington, after ruling that the states are likely to win their lawsuit claiming the move was illegal. The $5 billion program, part of the Inflation Reduction Act, was suspended by the Transportation Department in February, with previously approved spending plans rescinded. The ruling by Judge Tana Lin in Seattle excludes DC, Minnesota, and Vermont, which also sued but didnâ€™t show immediate harm. The order takes effect in seven days, giving time for an appeal. California's attorney general said the cut favored oil interests, while states argued the decision would hurt climate goals and green infrastructure. The case comes as Republicans push to reverse EV support, including a May bill to scrap a $7,500 EV tax credit, repeal emissions rules, and block new federal EV charger installations.
author: Analysts Account
